levels.title: Foundation
levels.subtitle: A1 from zero
levels.description: Start speaking from the first tiny patterns.

levels.title: A1 Core
levels.subtitle: Build daily survival Spanish
levels.description: More A1 grammar, listening, and practical routines.

levels.title: A2 Bridge
levels.subtitle: A1 to A2 transition
levels.description: Past, future, opinions, and fuller daily-life conversations.

levels.title: A2 Core
levels.subtitle: Strong A2 habits
levels.description: Narration, plans, problems, comparisons, and more natural interaction.

levels.title: B1 Bridge
levels.subtitle: A2 to B1
levels.description: Longer answers, stories, opinions, and native-style beginner input.

levels.title: B1 Core
levels.subtitle: Balanced B1 Spanish
levels.description: Speaking, writing, listening, reading, and interaction without silent-language gaps.

levels.title: B2 Bridge
levels.subtitle: B1 to B2
levels.description: Nuance, argument, abstract topics, native content, and output stamina.

levels.title: B2 Core
levels.subtitle: Independent Spanish
levels.description: Strong B2 expression across conversation, writing, media, and real-world tasks.

lessons.title: Sounds and first greetings
lessons.subtitle: Start with polite, repeatable Spanish sounds.

lessons.title: Subject pronouns + ser
lessons.subtitle: Say who someone is with soy, eres, es.

lessons.title: Ser for origin and profession
lessons.subtitle: Use ser for stable identity facts.

lessons.title: Estar for feelings and location
lessons.subtitle: Say how you are and where things are.

lessons.title: Articles and gender
lessons.subtitle: Learn nouns with el, la, un, una.

lessons.title: Plurals: -s and -es
lessons.subtitle: Make simple nouns plural.

lessons.title: Adjectives agree
lessons.subtitle: Make descriptions match the noun.

lessons.title: -ar verbs in the present
lessons.subtitle: Build many sentences with hablar.

lessons.title: -er and -ir verbs
lessons.subtitle: Add comer and vivir to your sentence engine.

lessons.title: Tener: have, age, need
lessons.subtitle: Use tengo for daily facts.

lessons.title: Hay: there is / there are
lessons.subtitle: Describe what exists in a place.

lessons.title: Question words
lessons.subtitle: Ask useful things immediately.

lessons.title: Yes/no questions
lessons.subtitle: Turn statements into questions with voice and punctuation.

lessons.title: Negation with no
lessons.subtitle: Say what is not true.

lessons.title: Me gusta / me gustan
lessons.subtitle: Say what you like without overthinking grammar.

lessons.title: Ir + a + place
lessons.subtitle: Say where you go.

lessons.title: Near future: ir a + infinitive
lessons.subtitle: Talk about what you are going to do.

lessons.title: Querer and poder
lessons.subtitle: Say what you want and can do.

lessons.title: Tener que + infinitive
lessons.subtitle: Say what you have to do.

lessons.title: Home nouns + tiny examples
lessons.subtitle: Name useful things around you.

lessons.title: Time words and days
lessons.subtitle: Place your sentence in time.

lessons.title: Numbers and prices
lessons.subtitle: Ask simple prices and quantities.

lessons.title: Estoy + -ando / -iendo
lessons.subtitle: Say what is happening right now.

lessons.title: Este, esta, esto
lessons.subtitle: Point to things around you.

lessons.title: Mi, tu, su
lessons.subtitle: Say my, your, his, her, their.

lessons.title: Lo and la for it
lessons.subtitle: Replace a thing after the verb.

lessons.title: Location words
lessons.subtitle: Put people and things in space.

lessons.title: Ordering food politely
lessons.subtitle: Use quiero and quisiera in a cafe.

lessons.title: Small talk: name, origin, mood
lessons.subtitle: Combine early patterns naturally.

lessons.title: Foundation review conversation
lessons.subtitle: Use the first 29 lessons in one small scene.
